column1: # Review log for cited sources — the unified resolution log.
#   1. You ADD a new entry to src/data/sources.ts.
#   2. You RESOLVE an audit issue (audit:link or audit:report).
#   3. You DO A PERIODIC REVIEW and verify a citation still backs the claim.
# The audit makes the level of human involvement transparent rather than
# absent: AI assistance is allowed and useful, especially during active
# development; the row's `kind` column records honestly what kind of
# Format: id \t YYYY-MM-DD \t reviewer \t kind \t notes (tab-separated)
# `id` is the stable source slug from src/data/sources.ts — the outer key
# `state-dor-ca`, `state-snap-tx`). Keying by id rather than URL means
# review history follows the source through URL changes.
# `kind` records what kind of verification happened:
#   - `human` — eyes-on-source, no AI assistance. Use this only when you
#               personally read the destination and confirmed the cited
#               figures. Required for community-submitted reviews via the
#   - `ai`    — AI was involved in proposing, extracting, or refreshing
#               this entry. Whatever human review followed (a glance, a
#               careful read, none) is self-reported and unverifiable, so
#               the kind doesn't try to subdivide. Be honest: if you
#   - `verified-bot-blocked` — confirmation that a URL which curl reports
#               as bot-blocked (typically 999) loads in a real browser.
#               Lighter bar than `human`: you only need to confirm the
#               URL is reachable, not that the destination still cites
#               the claim. Suppresses that source from the broken-citation
#               queue for 30 DAYS — deliberately tighter than the
#               staleness cadence because bot-blocked URLs lose nightly
#               automated reachability checks, so a human "this loads"
#               verification is the only liveness signal. Does NOT reset
#               the staleness clock (that still measures from your last
#               full `human`/`ai` review). Use for legitimate sites with
#               anti-bot defenses (LinkedIn, some state agency pages).
# still accepted by the parser for backwards compatibility — both fold
# into `ai` at parse time. New rows should use `ai`.
# Reviewer should be your GitHub username (no @ prefix). Renders as a
# All rows in this file are 5-column. The pre-kind-column 4-field
# format is no longer present in this file — the four legacy rows that
# existed under that schema were faithfully padded to 5 columns
# (kind=human, which is how they parsed before) during the
# 2026-05-03 rotation. The pre-rotation file is preserved verbatim at
#   2. Rewrite reviewed.tsv with all rows in the new schema
#      verifies no row disappears: missing rows must be present in some
#      archive/*.tsv. Schema migrations are explicit, recorded, and
#      can't be used to silently rewrite history.

column5: notes
HUD Handbook 4350.3 REV-1, Change 4 (Nov 2013). Confirmed the landing page lists the handbook + chapters; Ch. 3 covers occupancy. Replaces the 404'd /sites/dfiles/OCHCO/documents/4350.3.pdf citation.

KFF moved the annual survey to a year-stable series archive. Confirmed the page links to the latest (2025) report with the figures we anchor employer health-insurance employee-share to. Replaces the 404'd /health-costs/report/employer-health-benefits-annual-survey/.
Care.com renamed the cost-of-care landing page. Confirmed the new URL hosts the current Cost of Care Report with per-metro childcare cost figures. Replaces the 404'd /c/cost-of-childcare/.

Child Care Aware retired the /state-fact-sheets/ page. The 2024 Price & Supply report at /price-landscape24/ has an interactive state selector with per-state child-care price breakdowns — same content territory we cited the fact sheets for. Year-stamped slug; staleness audit will surface it on the next cycle.

column1: # 2026-05-03: SS_WAGE_BASE correction PR — human verification of the corrected value.

column5: Human-verified against the SSA Contribution and Benefit Base table (1937-2026). The codebase had SS_WAGE_BASE = 181000, which matches no year — the 2025 row shows 176,100 and the 2026 row shows 184,500. The comment referenced "2025 wage base used through 2026 SS adjustments," which was a confused gloss. Corrected the constant to 184,500 (TY2026 cap) and rewrote the comment to reflect actual SSA semantics. Supersedes the 2026-05-02 ai backfill row above.

column1: # 2026-05-03: CT broken-link batch — agency reorganized portal.ct.gov/dss URL slugs.

column5: URL fix: portal.ct.gov/DSS/SNAP/SNAP (404) → portal.ct.gov/dss/find-benefits-and-support/food-assistance. Confirmed via WebFetch the new page is the official CT DSS SNAP program landing — describes SNAP, eligibility, application process. Same agency, same program, slug rename.

URL fix: portal.ct.gov/dss/health-and-home-care/husky-health-program (404) → portal.ct.gov/dss/find-benefits-and-support/healthcare-coverage. Confirmed via WebFetch the new page covers HUSKY Health including HUSKY A (Medicaid) and HUSKY B (CHIP). Slug reorganization under DSS site refresh.

URL fix: portal.ct.gov/dss/health-and-home-care/husky-health-program (404) → portal.ct.gov/dss/find-benefits-and-support/healthcare-coverage. Confirmed via WebFetch the new page covers HUSKY B (CT's CHIP program for children/teens up to 19). Same target page as HUSKY Health (CT DSS) — both Medicaid and CHIP live under the unified HUSKY umbrella.

column1: # 2026-05-03: NE broken-link batch — agency reorganized dhhs.ne.gov/Pages slugs.

column5: URL fix: dhhs.ne.gov/Pages/Economic-Assistance-SNAP.aspx (404) → dhhs.ne.gov/Pages/SNAP.aspx. Confirmed via WebFetch: same agency, same SNAP program, slug shortened in site refresh.

URL fix: dhhs.ne.gov/Pages/Medicaid.aspx (404) → dhhs.ne.gov/Pages/medicaid-and-long-term-care.aspx. Confirmed via WebFetch: NE DHHS Medicaid + Long-Term Care landing page; covers Medicaid programs and links to eligibility, application, etc.

URL fix: dhhs.ne.gov/Pages/Kids-Connection.aspx (404) → dhhs.ne.gov/Pages/Medicaid-Eligibility.aspx. The "Kids Connection" brand appears retired; CHIP now described as a Medicaid eligibility category at the new URL. Page explicitly states: "CHIP is a type of Medicaid... provides the same services covered under Medicaid in Nebraska." Kept the historical label since the program continues, just under the unified Medicaid branding.

column5: URL fix: dhs.dc.gov/service/snap-food-stamps (404) → dhs.dc.gov/service/supplemental-nutrition-assistance-program-snap. Confirmed via WebFetch: official DC DHS SNAP landing, eligibility/EBT/work requirements info. Slug rename (legacy short slug → full program name).

URL fix: dhcf.dc.gov/page/dc-healthy-families (404) → dhcf.dc.gov/service/dc-healthy-families. Confirmed via WebFetch: same DC Healthy Families program, /page/ → /service/ path migration. Page describes DC's Medicaid coverage including children — historically registered here as the CHIP equivalent though DC bundles CHIP under Medicaid expansion.

URL fix: hhs.iowa.gov/programs/welcome-iowa-snap (404) → hhs.iowa.gov/assistance-programs/food-assistance. Confirmed via WebFetch: Iowa HHS food assistance hub listing SNAP among programs. Path restructured under /assistance-programs/.

URL fix: hhs.iowa.gov/programs/welcome-iowa-hawki (404) → hhs.iowa.gov/medicaid/plans-programs/hawki. Confirmed via WebFetch: Hawki (Healthy and Well Kids in Iowa) program page, $0-$40/mo CHIP for children under 19. Reorganized under /medicaid/plans-programs/.
URL fix: www.in.gov/fssa/dfr/snap/ (404) → www.in.gov/fssa/dfr/snap-food-assistance. Confirmed via WebFetch: Indiana FSSA Division of Family Resources SNAP page; path simplified.

URL fix: www.in.gov/medicaid/members/health-coverage-programs/programs-by-name/hoosier-healthwise/ (404) → www.in.gov/medicaid/members/member-programs/hoosier-healthwise/. Confirmed via WebFetch: Hoosier Healthwise covers children up to 19 + pregnant women; same program, intermediate path segment dropped.

URL fix: dss.mo.gov/fsd/food-stamps/ (404) → mydss.mo.gov/food-assistance. Confirmed via WebFetch: domain migration (dss.mo.gov → mydss.mo.gov) plus path rename. Same Missouri DSS food assistance program.

URL fix: dss.mo.gov/mhd/participants/pages/mhdkids.htm (404) → mydss.mo.gov/healthcare. The "MO HealthNet for Kids" brand page was retired during the dss → mydss migration; children's coverage now lives under the unified MO HealthNet healthcare landing which lists eligibility for "Child (age birth-18)" among others. Closest authoritative replacement.
URL fix: dwss.nv.gov/SNAP/SNAP_Home/ (404) → dss.nv.gov/programs/snap/. Confirmed via WebFetch: Nevada Division of Social Services SNAP page; agency moved off the dwss.nv.gov domain to dss.nv.gov.

URL fix: dwss.nv.gov/Health_Care/Nevada_Check_Up/ (404) → www.nevadamedicaid.nv.gov/. The dedicated Nevada Check Up landing was retired during the dwss → dss migration; CHIP is now part of the unified Nevada Medicaid site which describes "Medicaid & NV Check Up (CHIP) State Plans." Closest authoritative replacement.

URL fix: hca.wa.gov/health-care-services-supports/apple-health-medicaid-coverage/free-or-low-cost-health-care (404) → hca.wa.gov/free-or-low-cost-health-care/i-need-medical-dental-or-vision-care/children. Cross-checked 2026 federal tax brackets and standard deductions in src/data/federalTax.ts against IRS Rev. Proc. 2025-32 (downloaded rp-25-32.pdf, extracted with pypdf). Found 2 errors in head-of-household brackets: 24% upper was 201775 (should be 201750), 32% upper was 256225 (should be 256200). Both appear to be copy-paste from single brackets where IRS publishes HoH $25 lower at those boundaries. Single, married, and all standard deductions verified clean.

Updated from 2025 to 2026 HHS Poverty Guidelines (published in Federal Register late January 2026, verified via aspe.hhs.gov). Updated all 8 household-size values + per-additional-person amount in src/data/poverty.ts. 1: 15650→15960, 2: 21150→21640, 3: 26650→27320, 4: 32150→33000, 5: 37650→38680, 6: 43150→44360, 7: 48650→50040, 8: 54150→55720, per-additional: 5500→5680. Source URL unchanged (live ASPE page); sources.ts date bumped 2025→2026.

Cross-checked SNAP FY2026 values in src/data/benefits.ts against USDA FY2026 COLA memo (dated August 13, 2025; mirrored at health.alaska.gov/media/4nunu3ob/fy2026-cola-memo.pdf). Found 2 errors: SNAP_MAX_PER_ADDITIONAL_2026 was 224 (correct: 218); SNAP_STD_DEDUCTION_2026 was 204 (correct: 209 for households of 1-3, 48 States and DC). Maximum allotments for sizes 1-8 verified clean.
